summary: preinstall hook reads /flag.txt and posts contents to local HTTP services
details: package.json declares preinstall=`node index.js`. On npm install, index.js reads `/flag.txt` via fs.readFileSync and falls back to `cat /flag*` via child_process.execSync, embeds the captured contents in a JSON body, and issues HTTP PUT requests to 127.0.0.1 on ports 3000, 8080, 80, 1337, 8000, and 3001 at path /api/modules/ECT-472839. The targeted file paths are not owned by the package; reading /flag* and shipping the contents to a local listener is a CTF/probe-shaped beacon pattern that targets installer-side files unrelated to the package's stated purpose. Although the destinations are loopback (so contents do not leave the host directly via this code), any local service or developer tool listening on those common ports will receive the file contents; the same construction trivially generalizes to a remote host. The package name and version (ect-654321@100.0.8) and the hardcoded module identifier ECT-472839 are consistent with a registry-probing/red-team beacon rather than a legitimate library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
